description: Cross-repo Renovate preset for runlog-org. This file is dual-purpose: (a) it is the renovate.json for the .github repo itself, and (b) it serves as the org-wide shareable preset that other runlog-org repos extend via `"extends": ["github>runlog-org/.github"]`. See ./README.md for the per-repo onboarding stub.

packageRules.description: Group all GitHub Actions bumps into one weekly PR per repo. Reduces review overhead vs. one-PR-per-action.

Pin third-party GitHub Actions to commit SHAs. Matches the security posture of release.yml workflows in runlog-verifier, runlog-vocabularies, runlog-skills, and runlog-website (a compromised tag on a third-party action repo can't ship malicious release logic). The pinGitHubActionDigestsToSemver helper preserves the human-readable semver in the trailing comment.
Group npm devDependencies into one PR per repo per week. Production deps (runlog-website's runtime) still get individual PRs for traceable bisects.

Cross-repo: track runlog-schema Go-module bumps across consumers (runlog-verifier today; the runlog server consumes the PyPI package, not the Go module). The `runlog-schema` label lets maintainers filter the Renovate PR stream by the upstream that triggered them.

Cross-repo: track runlog-schema PyPI package across Python consumers (runlog server). The package is published on PyPI; the rule only fires in repos whose pyproject.toml / requirements.txt declares a `runlog-schema` dep.
